generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance SwingAngleEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Swing‑angle encoder providing real‑time angular position of the crane’s hoist"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SwingAngleReading
]
instance AnemometerSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Anemometer measuring wind speed to monitor external forces on the crane"
    DTDFVocab:producedData AnemometerReading
]
instance CartPositionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Cartesian position sensor on the gantry rail providing X‑axis location of the cart"
    DTDFVocab:producedData CartPositionReading
]
instance HoistPositionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Linear position sensor on the hoist rail providing Y‑axis location of the load"
    DTDFVocab:producedData HoistPositionReading
]

// C4: Physical‑to‑virtual interaction
instance SwingAngleReading : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SwingAngleEncoder
]
instance AnemometerReading : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom AnemometerSensor
]
instance CartPositionReading : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CartPositionSensor
]
instance HoistPositionReading : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HoistPositionSensor
]
instance OptimalControlCommand : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TrajectoryGeneratorContainer
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance CADGeometryModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "CAD‑derived geometric model of the gantry crane"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TrajectoryGeneratorContainer
]
instance KinematicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Kinematic model describing cart and hoist motion along X and Y axes"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TrajectoryGeneratorContainer, ValidationContainer
]
instance SwingAngleTimeseries : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time‑series database table for swing‑angle encoder data"
    DTDFVocab:fromData SwingAngleReading
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TimescaleDBContainer
]
instance AnemometerTimeseries : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time‑series database table for anemometer wind‑speed data"
    DTDFVocab:fromData AnemometerReading
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TimescaleDBContainer
]
instance CartPositionTimeseries : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time‑series database table for cart X‑axis position data"
    DTDFVocab:fromData CartPositionReading
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TimescaleDBContainer
]
instance HoistPositionTimeseries : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time‑series database table for hoist Y‑axis position data"
    DTDFVocab:fromData HoistPositionReading
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TimescaleDBContainer
]
instance ControlCommandTimeseries : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time‑series database table for control command events published via MQTT"
    DTDFVocab:fromData OptimalControlCommand
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TimescaleDBContainer
]

// C11: Enablers
instance TrajectoryGeneratorContainer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Docker container running the Trajectory Generator Service that computes optimal kinematic paths"
    DTDFVocab:enables OptimalControlService
]
instance ValidationContainer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Docker container running the Continuous Validation Service that compares simulation to live telemetry"
    DTDFVocab:enables ContinuousValidationService
]
instance MQTTBrokerContainer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Mosquitto MQTT broker streaming real‑time data between the twin and the physical crane"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService
]
instance TimescaleDBContainer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "TimescaleDB instance storing sensor and command time‑series data"
    DTDFVocab:enables HistoricalDataLoggingService
]
instance GrafanaContainer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Grafana container providing operator dashboards visualizing kinematics and performance metrics"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataVisualizationDashboardService
]

// C6: Services
instance OptimalControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Generates shortest‑path trajectories between specified rack positions while respecting joint velocity constraints and publishes control commands via MQTT"
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimalControlTrajectoryInsight, TrajectoryRecalculationAction
]
instance HistoricalDataLoggingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Records every sensor and command event in TimescaleDB, permitting post‑run analytics such as energy consumption and travel efficiency"
    DTDFVocab:provides HistoricalDataInsight, PostRunAnalyticsInsight
]
instance DataVisualizationDashboardService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides an operator dashboard that displays real‑time crane position, estimated remaining transit time, and flags anomalous events such as velocity spikes"
    DTDFVocab:provides DataVisualizationInsight, RealTimeKinematicInsight, AnomalyFlagInsight
]
instance ContinuousValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Computes the deviation between twin simulation and live measurements, yields a confidence score (0‑1) indicating twin fidelity, and triggers alerts when confidence falls below a pre‑set threshold"
    DTDFVocab:provides ConfidenceScoreInsight, ValidationAlertAction
]
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real‑time monitoring of the crane pose and actuator states"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeMonitoringInsight
]

]

    base:desc "Confidence score (0‑1) indicating twin fidelity to live telemetry"
]

]

    base:desc "Flags anomalous events such as velocity spikes detected by the Twin"
]

    base:desc "Post‑run analytics on energy consumption and travel efficiency derived from historical data"
]

    base:desc "Insight summarizing real‑time monitoring of crane pose and actuator states"
]

    base:desc "Operator can approve or override generated trajectories"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

    base:desc "Operator can request immediate re‑calculation of trajectory under exceptional circumstances"

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Operational commands originate from the Control Application and are dispatched through the Physical Crane Interface, a Python program that translates high‑level requests into hardware‑level commands sent to the crane’s controllers. The documentation does not describe communication protocols, authentication, or command confirmation mechanisms."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The system orchestrates its digital twin through a constellation of loosely coupled services, each deployed in a Docker container for reproducibility and isolation. A physical gantry crane equipped with an on‑board small‑board computer (e.g., Raspberry Pi) streams telemetry—position, speed, actuator states, and sensor readings—to a dedicated time‑series database (InfluxDB or TimescaleDB) that persists all raw measurements with timestamp precision. The MQTT broker acts as the central message bus: it forwards real‑time command and status topics such as '/crane/command' and '/crane/telemetry'. The Trajectory Generator Service subscribes to '/crane/telemetry', computes next waypoints, and publishes them to '/crane/trajectory'. The Simulation Service consumes these trajectory commands, runs a physics‑based model of the crane (implemented in a Python module), and outputs simulated kinematics to '/simulation/state', enabling comparison with actual measurements. The Continuous Validation Service listens to both '/crane/telemetry' and '/simulation/state', applies statistical similarity tests (e.g., sliding‑window correlation or mean‑squared error thresholds) to detect drifts, and writes validation flags back to '/validation/alert'. A web‑based Dashboard aggregates data from the database, subscribes to MQTT topics, and visualises real‑time telemetry, simulated states, and validation alerts via websockets. Resource management is handled by Docker Compose, which defines volume mounts to persist database data across restarts and controls CPU affinities to guarantee deterministic container start‑up order, ensuring that the simulation service only receives trajectories after the trajectory generator has initialised. Together, this constellation delivers a cohesive, event‑driven digital twin that synchronises physical actions with simulation and validation, while keeping each component independently maintainable."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "Services within the digital twin communicate via an MQTT broker (used for signaling between services) and the TimescaleDB database. The document does not provide details on network protocols beyond MQTT, network topology, bandwidth, latency, reliability, or security protocols. "
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "All components are executed on a single personal computer, with the Database, MQTT Broker, and Dashboard packaged as Docker containers to simplify deployment. The Physical Crane Interface, Control Application, and Logging Application run natively on the host OS. No cloud or distributed deployment is described; the system is intended for on‑premises, edge‑based operation."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The system adopts an ad‑hoc approach with no adherence to a commercial digital‑twin framework. No explicit compliance with industrial digital‑twin standards such as AutomationML or CIM is reported. The twin relies on open‑source technologies that follow standard protocols (e.g., MQTT for messaging, PostgreSQL/TimescaleDB for persistence, Docker for containerization). CAD models are produced in Autodesk Fusion 360, and system‑level models are built in Capella, but they are not mapped to an established industrial standard. No certification or regulatory framework is cited in the documentation."
]
